title: Optimizing workflow with "My Work"
excerpt: To minimize misunderstandings and time wasted by having multiple people on a task, we have chosen to assign an owner to all items/tasks, with one or more collaborators. This way, there is no doubt about who is responsibl…

posts.author: MartinWibe (Martin Wibe)
posts.cookedHtml:
To minimize misunderstandings and time wasted by having multiple people on a task, we have chosen to assign an owner to all items/tasks, with one or more collaborators. This way, there is no doubt about who is responsible, as well as who might only contribute with advice, guidance, or other relevant input, as one can clearly see their collaborators on the task.

We use “My Work” a lot as it provides a really good overview of one’s workday and acts as a to-do list for what needs to be accomplished for the day and the week. Unfortunately, currently in “My Work,” both owners and collaborators are seen as “people.” This leads to misunderstandings in our busy day-to-day about who actually owns the task.

Could there be a solution in “My Work” where one can choose which columns to select in relation to “people” so that it only includes the owner and not the collaborators?

Best regards

posts.author: VirtualVines (Roberto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys!

Yes, we have the solution. Our app, MyWeek, allows you to view activities in a calendar view of a week and choose the column people from your boards.

Key Features:

  • Centralize all activities in one dynamic weekly view
  • Customize your week to start on any day
  • Hide weekends for streamlined work planning
  • Group activities by project boards for perfect organization
  • Effortlessly drag and resize schedule items
  • Personalize your view with flexible columns (date/timeline, people & status)
  • Create and edit activities directly within the app
  • Navigate instantly to any day or week with our intuitive calendar
